rulings.Case Title: LEGALINK INC (FKA COMBS AND GREENLEY, INC) VS. JAY JACKMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Assignment Order And For Restraining Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 9, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF COMBS AND GREENLEY, INC.'S Motion For Assignment Order And For Restraining Order. Granted in full. Assignment is proper under Code of Civil Procedure sections 708.510 and 708.520. Plaintiff is not seeking assignment of wages paid from an employer to Defendant. A restraining order is proper under Code of Civil Procedure section 708.520 and necessary to ensure compliance with the assignment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LEGALINK INC (FKA COMBS AND GREENLEY, INC) VS. JAY JACKMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Combs And Greenley, Inc'S Motion For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 9, 2012, Line 9, Plaintiff Combs And Greenley, Inc'S Motion For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Jim Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted. Defendant shall pay a monetary sanction of $1,802.50 to Plaintiff within 15 calendar days of the entry of this order. The imposition of monetary sanctions for a party's refusal to comply with a previous sanctions order is provided for by, inter alia, Code Civ. Proc. §2030.290(c) and 2023.030(a). Defendant's request for monetary sanctions is denied. And even if defendant's position were justified, which it is not, an attorney acting in propria persona may not recover attorney's fees as a discovery sanction. Argaman v. Ratan, 73 Cal.App.4th 1173, 1175 (1999). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: DISCOVER BANK FKA GREENWOOD TRUST COMPANY VS. DENNES D HERNANDEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 9, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF DISCOVER BANK FKA GREENWOOD TRUST COMPANY HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION Pro Tem Judge Jim Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The claim of exemption is granted in part and denied in part. Although the judgment debtor did not complete the financial statement in support of his claim of exemption, the documents he provided in support demonstrate that the amount garnished exceeds the limitations of Code Civ. Proc. §§706.050 and 704.070 and 15 U.S.C. §1673(a). The documents provided by the judgment debtor indicate that he earned $1,119.45 in disposable income in the week of the garnishment. Therefore, only $279.86 is subject to execution. The claim of exemption is denied as to that amount and is granted as to the remaining funds garnished. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: NIKOLAS LEMOS VS. PROGRESSIVE CHOICE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Stay Discovery Pending Resolution Of Uim Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 9, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT PROGRESSIVE CHOICE INSURANCE COMPANY Notice Of Motion To Stay Discovery Pending Resolution Of Uim Arbitration Pro Tem Judge Jim Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied. Rather than moving for a protective order or other protection against discovery of specific matters, defendant seeks an across-the-board stay on discovery. That is overly broad and would undermine the usual presumption that discovery will be allowed to proceed in any sequence. Code Civ. Proc. §2019.020(a). Defendant's concerns about the disclosure of privileged or otherwise protected information in a few unspecified instances do not outweigh this policy and do not justify an overall stay on discovery. If there are particular discovery requests that might be objectionable, defendant should have moved for a protective order as to those matters. Defendant's arguments concerning Code Civ. Proc. §1281.4 are not well-taken. That statute pertains to contractual arbitration generally; however, the Court notes that the UIM statute specifically provides that "[n]othing in this section limits the rights of any party to discovery in any action pending or that may hereafter be pending in any court." Ins. Code §11580.2(f)(7). The more specific statute prevails over the more general provisions of §1281.4. Code Civ. Proc. §1859; Collection Bureau of San Jose v. Rumsey, 24 Cal.4th 301, 310 (2000). The UIM statute provides specifically for discovery to proceed in a civil action without regard to the pendency of the UIM arbitration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: NIKOLAS LEMOS VS. PROGRESSIVE CHOICE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Top Compel Further Responses From Progressive Choice Insruance Company Re Interrogatories ; Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 9, 2012, Line 5, PLAINTIFF NIKOLAS LEMOS' Notice Of Motion And Motion Top Compel Further Responses From Progressive Choice Insruance Company Re Interrogatories Plaintiff Nicholas Lemos' Motion To Compel Further Responses From Progressive Choice Insurance Co. Re Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Jim Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The parties shall provide an update at hearing as to any further responses or production since the filing of plaintiff's reply papers. Subject to any such update: The motion is granted as to Requests for Production Nos. 2 (as limited by plaintiff in its reply separate statement of items in dispute), 3 (as so limited), 3(2), 6, 7, 8 (as so limited), 9, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, and 16 (as limited by plaintiff in the same regard as nos. 2, 3, and 8); and Form Interrogatory No. 17.1. The motion is also granted as to the production of materials relating to office-specific or region-specific claims procedures. The motion is granted as to Requests for Production Nos. 4 and 5 only insofar as those requests call for documents concerning training, education, and performance, and as limited by the protective order agreed to by the parties. The motion is denied as to all other requests.

rulings.Case Title: NIKOLAS LEMOS VS. PROGRESSIVE CHOICE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Nicholas Lemos' Motion To Compel Further Responses From Progressive Choice Insurance Co. Re Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 9, 2012, Line 5, Plaintiff Nicholas Lemos' Motion To Compel Further Responses From Progressive Choice Insurance Co. Re Interrogatories - (part 2) - Defendant's arguments about the alleged prejudice to the UIM arbitration are not well taken. The UIM statute specifically provides for discovery to proceed in a civil action notwithstanding the pendency of a UIM arbitration proceeding. Ins. Code §11580.2(f)(7). Defendant shall provide further responses within 20 calendar days of the entry of this order, without objections except as to privilege. If defendant contends that any responsive document is privileged, defendant shall produce a privilege log stating the nature of the document, its date, its author, the recipient(s) thereof, its sequence or identification number if any, and the privilege claimed as to that document. Code Civ. Proc. §2031.240(b); Best Products, Inc. v. Superior Court, 119 Cal.App.4th 1181, 1188 (2004). If it turns out that a particular document does not exist or cannot be found, those facts shall be stated accordingly in the response. Plaintiff's request for sanctions of $1,000.00 is granted. Defendant shall pay said amount to plaintiff's counsel within 20 calendar days of the entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: LUIS ADOLFO MARQUEZ et al VS. PG & E et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Leave To File A Complaint In Intervention; Memo Of P & Dec. And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 9, 2012, Line 11, INTERVENOR ZURICH NORTH AMERICA'S Motion For Leave To File A Complaint In Intervention. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ABS SEAFOOD, INC., A CORPORATION VS. FARINA FOCACCIA CUCINA ITALIANA, LLC, A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 9, 2012, Line 7, PLAINTIFF ABS SEAFOOD, INC., A CORPORATION'S Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment Pro Tem Judge Jim Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: There being no opposition to the application, the application is granted. See Code Civ. Proc. §484.060(a). A right to attach order and a writ of attachment will issue in the form to be provided by the moving party. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.
